chat.title: Cortex
chat.empty: Ask the assistant anything. It can call your enabled plugin tools.
chat.keyPrompt: Add your API key to start chatting. It is stored encrypted on this device and never leaves the main process.

onboarding.headline: Your desktop AI companion
onboarding.subtitle: Connect an API key to chat, use plugin tools, hook up MCP servers, and build long-term memory.
onboarding.featurePluginsDesc: Run tasks through your enabled plugins
onboarding.featureMcpDesc: Extend the assistant with external tools
onboarding.featureMemoryDesc: Import documents the assistant can recall
onboarding.featureSecureDesc: Keys stay on this device and never leave the main process
onboarding.prompt1: Help me organize this week's todos
onboarding.prompt2: Search and launch a local app

chat.startFailed: The assistant could not start this turn. Please try again.

providers.title: AI settings
providers.subtitle: Choose your provider and model (BYOK). Keys are stored encrypted on this device and never leave the main process.
providers.keyHint: Each provider keeps its own key; switching providers does not clear it.
providers.budgetHint: Stop a turn once it spends this many tokens. Leave empty or 0 for no limit.
providers.contextCompressionHint: When enabled, older conversation turns are summarized before sending to the model once the estimated token count exceeds the threshold.
providers.toolResilienceHint: Circuit breaker for tool calls: a tool is short-circuited after this many consecutive failures, then retried after the recovery window. Timeout 0 disables it; local execution and subagent tools are never timed out.

memory.title: Long-term memory
memory.subtitle: Import documents to make their passages searchable by the assistant, and review or delete what is remembered.
memory.deleteSourceBody: This removes all chunks from "{{source}}". This cannot be undone.

mcp.title: MCP servers
mcp.subtitle: Connect external Model Context Protocol servers. Their tools become available to the assistant, gated by the same approval rules.
mcp.deleteConfirmBody: Remove "{{id}}"? The assistant will no longer use its tools.
mcp.idHint: Stable identifier (letters, numbers, - and _). Used to namespace tools.
mcp.name: Name
mcp.urlHint: Streamable HTTP endpoint; SSE is used as a fallback.
mcp.headersHint: KEY=value, one per line. Avoid storing secrets here.
mcp.commandHint: Executable to launch, e.g. npx or node.
mcp.envHint: KEY=value, one per line. Avoid storing secrets here.

roots.label: Expose execution roots
roots.hint: Off by default. Directories checked here are reported to this server as MCP roots — only pick ones you're comfortable this server knowing the path to.

frequentApps.title: Frequent Apps
frequentApps.subtitle: Apps you've launched recently — open them again in one click.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.updatesEmpty: All installed plugins are up to date.
plugins.trendingEmpty: No marketplace plugins to show right now.

cortex.title: Cortex
cortex.emptyHint: No conversations yet — start your first one.

recommendations.title: Today's Picks
recommendations.placeholder: Cortex is learning how you work — personalized suggestions are coming soon.

agentShell.title: Allow local execution
agentShell.description: High-risk. The assistant can read files, apply patches, and run local commands inside authorized workspaces. Writes and commands require your confirmation, stay confined to allowed workspaces, and system-level or catastrophic commands are refused.
agentShell.rootsMovedNotice: Manage which folders are authorized from each workspace's own settings.

workspaceRoots.title: Manage roots
workspaceRoots.empty: No roots configured for this workspace.
workspaceRoots.disabledNotice: Turn on "Allow local execution" in Settings before adding roots.

workspaceSettings.title: Workspaces
workspaceSettings.subtitle: Rename or archive workspaces. Archiving hides a workspace from the switcher and pauses its background triggers, without deleting anything.
workspaceSettings.defaultWorkspaceHint: The default workspace can't be renamed or archived.
workspaceSettings.archiveConfirmBody: External MCP clients using this workspace ID will no longer be able to read its resources or call its tools. They'll need a different workspace ID, or you can unarchive this one later.

mcpOnboarding.title: Connect an MCP client
mcpOnboarding.subtitle: Generate a Claude Desktop configuration for this workspace, or test that the connection actually works.
mcpOnboarding.devNote: Config generation and connection testing require a packaged build.
mcpOnboarding.archivedNote: This workspace is archived. Unarchive it to connect an MCP client.

trustedSources.title: Trusted plugin sources
trustedSources.subtitle: Choose which plugin package sources Synapse exposes in the desktop app.

policy.officialMarketplaceHint: Show only packages resolved by the Synapse marketplace backend.
policy.anyUrlHint: Allow marketplace and URL-backed package sources exposed by the client.
policy.localSynHint: Only expose local package import from files on this device.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.subtitle: Switch between light and dark, or pick an accent color.

settings.title: Floating Ball
settings.subtitle: Show a desktop shortcut ball and choose which tools appear in its radial menu.
settings.enableHint: Disabled by default at startup until you turn it on here.
settings.menuFeaturesHint: Choose up to 6 tools. The floating ball itself is not a menu tool.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.subtitle: Manage built-in, installed, and development plugins.
plugins.unavailableBody: Plugin management is only available inside the desktop app.
plugins.emptyBody: No built-in, installed, or development plugins are registered yet.
plugins.filteredEmptyBody: Try another search term, source, or status filter.
plugins.searchPlaceholder: Search plugins, authors, commands, or permissions…

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.body: Changes are saved to the plugin preference store.

connectDialog.title: Connect GitHub account
connectDialog.description: Synapse will open GitHub authorization in your browser. After you approve, this dialog continues automatically.
connectDialog.githubInboxDescription: Connect GitHub so GitHub Inbox can read notifications and run confirmed writeback actions.
connectDialog.waiting: After authorization, Synapse encrypts and stores the credential, then connects the plugin.
connectDialog.secureHint: Access tokens are encrypted in the main process; the plugin sandbox never sees the raw secret.
connectDialog.githubScopes: This plugin requests notifications, read:user, and repo to read notifications and perform confirmed writebacks in repositories you can access.

capabilities.preauthorizeLabel: Skip the approval prompt for external MCP calls
capabilities.preauthorizeWarning: Allows any external MCP client able to launch Synapse's local MCP connection to call this capability without a per-call prompt.
capabilities.reportedIdentity: Reported identity: {{clientId}} (self-reported by the client, not verified)
capabilities.requestCancelledBody: The request behind this prompt was cancelled elsewhere, so there's nothing left to approve.

hostResources.approvalBody: An external MCP client wants to read {{resourceLabel}} for workspace {{workspaceName}} (root: {{rootName}}).
hostResources.reportedIdentity: Reported identity: {{clientId}} (self-reported by the client, not verified)

mcpExposure.toggleLabel: Expose non-read-only tools to external MCP clients
mcpExposure.warning: Turns on external visibility for every non-read-only tool this plugin has (including ones marked destructive). Whether a call still needs per-call confirmation depends on the capability it uses — a tool that uses no managed capability at all would be callable with no prompt.

triggers.enableConfirmTitle: Let this plugin run in the background?
triggers.enableConfirmBody: Once enabled, this plugin can act on its own in the background — using the permissions listed below — without asking each time. You can pause or stop it anytime, and built-in limits cap how often it runs.
triggers.clipboardDisclosureBody: While enabled, this plugin notices when you copy something. It only learns that the clipboard changed unless you also let it read the contents.
triggers.fsWatchDisclosureBody: While enabled, this plugin notices when files are added or change in the folders listed below.
triggers.hotkeyDisclosureBody: While enabled, this plugin's keyboard shortcut works everywhere — even when Synapse isn't in focus. System shortcuts (copy, paste, quit, Alt+F4, etc.) and the launcher shortcut are always protected.
triggers.hotkeyCaptureWarning: Captures {{accelerator}} everywhere while enabled, even when Synapse isn't in focus.
triggers.noInstances: Not active in any workspace yet.
triggers.agentActivationNote: After enabling, this automation must be activated per workspace from the Active Background panel.
triggers.migrationNoticeTitle: Background automations now run per workspace
triggers.migrationNoticeBody: These automations were running before this update and need to be re-activated per workspace: {{triggers}}
triggers.pendingCapabilityBody: {{plugin}} wants to use: {{capabilities}}. Review and approve to let it start using them.

items.credentials:broker: Use connected accounts on your behalf
items.memory:read: Read this workspace's memory and global memory

summary.credentialsBrokered: Credentials are held by Synapse; the plugin cannot read your token
summary.credentialsBrokeredPending: May use brokered credentials after you connect and grant access
summary.backgroundPending: Has background automation (not active until capabilities are granted)
summary.localReadPending: May read local files in allowed folders (not yet granted)
summary.agentCallable: Exposes {{count}} tool(s) the agent can call

warning.remoteWriteback: Can write back to remote services; writeback requires your confirmation
warning.remoteWritebackPending: May write back to remote services once network access is granted
warning.localWrite: Can write to local files in the folders you allow
warning.localWritePending: May write local files once write access is granted
warning.unknownCapability: Declares a capability this version does not recognize
warning.ungrantedCapabilities: Some declared capabilities are not granted yet

marketplace.title: Marketplace
marketplace.subtitle: Discover and install Synapse plugins.
marketplace.unavailableBody: The marketplace is only available inside the desktop app.
marketplace.loadingHint: Fetching the registry and checking installed plugins.
marketplace.empty: No marketplace plugins match your filters.
marketplace.protectedSource: This plugin is provided by {{source}} and cannot be reinstalled from the marketplace.

governance.emptyMine: No plugins published by this account.
governance.signInRequired: Sign in to manage your marketplace plugins.
governance.defaultReportReason: Reported from the Synapse desktop app.

review.title: Review queue
review.subtitle: Open reports — from users and the automated upload scan.

account.prompt: Complete GitHub authorization in your browser.
account.dialogDescription: Synapse opened the GitHub authorization page in your browser. After you authorize, this window will continue automatically.
account.waiting: After approval, Synapse will connect your marketplace account automatically.
account.secureHint: The app only receives a marketplace session after GitHub confirms the request.

marketplace.comingSoonBody: The sidebar entry is ready; Git registry browsing and install flows will land in the marketplace PR.
marketplace.gitIntegrationHint: P0 marketplace installation will use HTTPS registry fetches and package hash verification instead of a local git dependency.

lan.title: LAN Transfer
lan.subtitle: Find nearby devices on the same network and send files quickly.
lan.unavailableBody: LAN transfer is available in the Synapse desktop app.
lan.restartRequired: Restart Synapse to use LAN transfer. Close the app and open it again.
lan.securityBody: When connecting to a new device for the first time, both devices show a security code. Confirm that the numbers match before sending files. You can choose whether to save received files.

discovery.title: Find nearby devices
discovery.subtitle: When enabled, other Synapse devices on the same network can find this device, and you can see them here.

devices.title: Nearby devices
devices.subtitle: Before sending files to a new device, confirm a security code once.
devices.emptyBody: Make sure the other device is on the same network and has “Find nearby devices” enabled in Synapse.

pairings.title: Confirm new device
pairings.subtitle: The device you are connecting to shows a six-digit code. Enter it here to connect.
pairings.showCodeHint: Enter this code on the device that started the connection.
pairings.enterCodeBody: Enter the six-digit code shown on {{name}} to connect.
pairings.invalidCode: That code is incorrect. Check the other device and try again.

transfers.title: File transfers
transfers.subtitle: Track sent and received files here. Interrupted transfers can resume.
transfers.emptyBody: Connect to a nearby device, then choose a file to send.

runObservatory.title: Runs
runObservatory.subtitle: Browse the latest 500 agent runs. History retains the latest 500 runs.
runObservatory.emptyList: No runs match the current filters.
runObservatory.selectPrompt: Select a run to see its details.
runObservatory.parentUnavailable: Parent run trace is unavailable. It may have aged out of retention or failed to persist.
runObservatory.recoverableConflict: Conversation conflict — this run can only be abandoned.

settings.title: Launcher
settings.subtitle: Summon the command palette from anywhere with a global shortcut.
settings.hotkeyHint: You can manually enter Electron shortcut syntax, or click to capture and then press the key combination. Shortcut combinations that are already in use may not be captured; please use unused combinations or enter them manually.
settings.invalid: Could not register that combination. The previous shortcut is still active.
settings.resumeFailed: The hotkey couldn't be restored — it may now be in use by another app.
